stories.headline: Anthropic launches Claude for Small Business at $30/month per user
stories.blurb: Small teams can now get Claude Team features — shared billing, centralized admin, and usage analytics — at half the price of the full Team plan. The tier is aimed at businesses with fewer than 10 employees who want collaboration tools without enterprise overhead.
stories.why_it_matters: Makes AI collaboration accessible to the smallest companies at a price point they can actually afford.

stories.headline: Google's Genie can now turn Street View photos into playable 3D worlds
stories.blurb: Google AI Ultra subscribers can now feed Project Genie a Street View image and get back an interactive 3D environment they can walk through. The AI generates a simulated world from a single photo, letting you explore real places as if you're there—no game engine required.
stories.why_it_matters: AI can now turn any photograph into a space you can move around in.
stories.original_headline: Simulate real-world places with Project Genie and Street View

stories.headline: Cursor now gives AI agents their own dev environments in the cloud
stories.blurb: Cursor just launched cloud dev environments for its AI agents — complete with cloned repos, installed dependencies, credentials, and build tools. The setup mirrors what you'd have on your laptop, so agents can handle full engineering tasks without bouncing back to your local machine.
stories.why_it_matters: AI coding agents can now finish tasks end-to-end without needing your actual computer running.

stories.headline: This indie app tries to be the AI assistant that knows what you need before you ask
stories.blurb: Poppy is a new iOS app that aims to surface helpful context proactively instead of waiting for you to prompt it. Unlike Siri or ChatGPT, which respond to requests, Poppy monitors your digital life and tries to offer relevant information before you realize you need it.
stories.why_it_matters: Shows how indie developers are experimenting with ambient AI that anticipates rather than reacts.
stories.original_headline: Indie App Spotlight: ‘Poppy’ is a proactive AI assistant that handles what Siri still can’t

try_this_prompt.title: The confidence tax
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm about to paste something I wrote. Don't improve it. Tell me which sentences exist only because I was worried you'd think I'm stupid, which clauses are pre-emptive apologies, and which phrases are doing everyone's thinking for them. Then show me the same thing with all that stripped out.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: When your draft feels long but you can't find what to cut.

daily_question: If an AI could see everything on your screen right now, what's the first thing you'd want it to do?
notes:
Initial review:
- Cursor stories (32, 39, 37) dominate engineer tier - vendor cap allows 2 max
- #32 (Cursor funding) is traditional funding news, lacks concrete try-it hook
- #39 (cloud dev environments for agents) has strong capability angle - agents work while you sleep
- #11 (Claude Small Business) is SMB tier launch - needs to verify concrete hook beyond pricing
- #54 (Genie + Street View) is cool world-building capability
- #31 (Magnus Carlsen) is celebrity ambassador news - fun but thin on AI capability
- #6 (Poppy) is proactive assistant for Mac - addresses mobile/consumer gap
- #53 (cellular aging) is DeepMind research application - strong cross-industry angle

Pool quality: Heavy on Cursor changelog, thin on consumer/mobile breakthroughs. Need to verify which stories have genuine wow factor vs incremental.
